location_definitions.label: Repo-tracked source and config files
location_definitions.rationale: No raw provisioning secret may be committed to source control.

location_definitions.label: Markdown docs and task evidence
location_definitions.rationale: Human-readable docs may retain aliases or refs only, never raw secret values.

location_definitions.label: CI stdout and stderr
location_definitions.rationale: Pipeline output is too broad and too durable for raw provisioning secrets.

location_definitions.label: General logs
location_definitions.rationale: Logs may contain metadata only and must not become bearer-secret storage.

location_definitions.label: Playwright trace archives
location_definitions.rationale: Trace bundles can replay form state and visible secret-entry surfaces.

location_definitions.label: Screenshots
location_definitions.rationale: Secret-entry and one-time reveal screens must default to suppression.

location_definitions.label: DOM snapshots
location_definitions.rationale: Rendered HTML can contain passwords, activation links, OTPs, cookies, or hidden tokens.

location_definitions.label: HTML reports
location_definitions.rationale: Aggregated reports are broad-read artifacts and are not a secret store.

location_definitions.label: Shell history and terminal transcripts
location_definitions.rationale: Manual recovery and copy-paste activity must not leak into operator shell history.

location_definitions.label: Unmanaged browser local storage, session storage, or cookies
location_definitions.rationale: Browser storage may contain session-bearing material and is outside the vault boundary.

location_definitions.label: Chat, ticketing, or email summaries
location_definitions.rationale: Informal channels are not approved capture channels for raw secret material.

location_definitions.label: Downloads, clipboard history, and temporary operator files
location_definitions.rationale: Temporary local surfaces are treated as contaminated unless explicitly purged.
